cards.title: Github Copilot Shifts to Token-Based Billing Model
cards.body: Microsoft's Github Copilot is moving away from its previous pricing structure toward token-based billing, sparking significant backlash from developers. The change marks a notable shift in the company's AI assistant strategy.

cards.title: Meta Developing AI-Powered Hardware Pendant
cards.body: Meta is reportedly building an AI pendant as part of its broader hardware ambitions. The move signals the company's commitment to wearable AI devices.

cards.title: Google's Gemini Spark Automates Daily Tasks
cards.body: Google's new 24/7 AI assistant Gemini Spark handles inbox summaries and local event planning effectively. Questions remain about why Google positioned it as a separate product.

cards.title: Browser Wars Heat Up Beyond Chrome, Safari
cards.body: Multiple alternative browsers are challenging Chrome and Safari dominance in 2026. The competitive landscape is expanding with new contenders.

cards.title: Coders Depend on AI Despite Quality Concerns
cards.body: Developers increasingly refuse to work without AI tools, yet research suggests AI-generated code may not be higher quality. This dependency could create long-term problems.

cards.title: Essential AI Terms and Definitions Glossary
cards.body: The rise of AI has introduced numerous new terminology and industry jargon. A comprehensive glossary helps decode the most important AI-related words and phrases.

cards.title: CEOs' AI Psychosis Leads to Reckless Decisions
cards.body: Box founder Aaron Levie warns that executives replacing jobs with AI often lack understanding of actual job responsibilities. ClickUp exemplifies this trend, cutting 22% of workforce for AI agents.

cards.title: Groq Chip Startup Raises $650M Funding Round
cards.body: Following Nvidia's $20 billion acquisition of Groq, the chipmaker is raising $650 million internally. Groq is shifting focus from hardware to AI inference optimization.

cards.title: Cognition's Devin Agent Won't Replace Developers
cards.body: Scott Wu, creator of Devin AI coding agent, emphasizes it's designed to augment rather than replace human programmers. Cognition maintains the tool supports developer productivity.

cards.title: TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 Speaker Applications Close
cards.body: Today is the final deadline to apply for speaking slots at TechCrunch Disrupt 2026. Submit your session topic to help shape industry-defining conversations.

cards.title: TechCrunch Disrupt Early Bird Savings Expire Tonight
cards.body: Attendees have until 11:59 p.m. PT today to secure Early Bird pricing of up to $410 off. Over 10,000 tech leaders expected at October's anticipated event.

cards.title: Kiwibit Smart Bird Feeder Gamifies Backyard Birding
cards.body: Kiwibit's AI-powered bird feeder combines nature observation with mobile gaming mechanics. Users can collect bird species on their app similar to Pokémon.

cards.title: XCENA Raises $135M on AI Memory Bottleneck Theory
cards.body: South Korean chip startup XCENA argues AI's primary constraint is memory, not compute power. The company secured $135 million backing for this thesis.

cards.title: Glean Triples Revenue with AI Cost-Cutting Focus
cards.body: Enterprise AI search startup Glean surpassed $300M in annual revenue amid tech budget cuts. The company tripled revenue while competing against major tech giants.

cards.title: Internet Infrastructure Rebuilt for AI Agents
cards.body: AWS, Cloudflare, and cloud providers are redesigning infrastructure for machine-generated traffic as AI agents move to production. The shift prioritizes machine-to-machine interactions over human users.

cards.title: Asana Acquires No-Code Agent Builder StackAI
cards.body: Asana integrated StackAI into its expanding AI workflow tools suite. The acquisition strengthens Asana's AI-powered productivity capabilities.

cards.title: Anthropic Closes $65B Series H at $965B Valuation
cards.body: Anthropic completed a $65 billion funding round approaching $1 trillion valuation. The round could be the AI startup's final private raise before anticipated IPO.

cards.title: AI Token Futures Trading Coming to Major Exchanges
cards.body: Large exchanges are designing derivative products around AI tokens as raw material inputs. AI tokens are increasingly treated like gold, oil, or electricity.

cards.title: StrictlyVC Los Angeles Conference Launching June 18
cards.body: StrictlyVC Los Angeles brings together tech leaders for networking and fireside chats. Event features executives from Mach Industries and Shinkei Systems.

cards.title: Github Copilot's Token Billing Sparks Developer Backlash
cards.body: Microsoft's Github Copilot is shifting to token-based billing, ending its free tier and frustrating developers. The move marks the end of the AI coding assistant's golden age of accessibility.

cards.title: Meta Develops AI-Powered Hardware Pendant
cards.body: Meta is making significant hardware bets on artificial intelligence technology. The company continues expanding beyond software into AI-enabled devices.

cards.title: Google's Gemini Spark 24/7 AI Assistant Reviewed
cards.body: Gemini Spark automates everyday tasks like inbox summaries and event planning with practical usefulness. Google's decision to launch it as a separate product remains unclear.

cards.title: Coders Depend on AI But Quality Concerns Rise
cards.body: While AI helps developers write code faster, researchers warn it may not produce better code. This dependency could create long-term problems for software quality.

cards.title: Essential AI Terms and Glossary Explained
cards.body: The AI boom has introduced countless new terms and slang that confuse many professionals. This glossary defines the most important words and phrases you'll encounter.

cards.title: CEOs Suffer from 'AI Psychosis' Says Box Founder
cards.body: Box founder Aaron Levie warns that executives replacing jobs with AI often don't understand those roles. ClickUp's 22% workforce cut for AI agents exemplifies this misunderstanding.

cards.title: Groq Raises $650M Amid AI Chip Competition
cards.body: Chipmaker Groq is securing $650 million in funding as it shifts focus to AI inference optimization. The company is pivoting from hardware manufacturing to refining AI model responses.

cards.title: Cognition's Devin AI Agent Shouldn't Replace Humans
cards.body: Scott Wu of Cognition, maker of the successful Devin AI coding agent, insists it's not designed to supplant human programmers. The tool aims to augment rather than eliminate developers.

cards.title: XCENA Chip Startup Raises $135M on Memory Hypothesis
cards.body: South Korean chip startup XCENA believes AI's true bottleneck is memory, not compute power. The company raised $135 million to pursue this alternative hardware approach.

cards.title: Glean Enterprise AI Search Crosses $300M Revenue
cards.body: Enterprise AI search startup Glean tripled annual revenue despite competition from tech giants. Budget-conscious AI solutions have become the company's major selling point.

cards.title: Internet Rebuilt for AI Agent Machine Traffic
cards.body: AWS, Cloudflare, and other cloud providers are redesigning infrastructure for AI agents moving into production. The shift prioritizes machine-generated traffic over human user interaction.

cards.title: Asana Acquires StackAI No-Code Agent Builder
cards.body: Asana incorporated StackAI into its growing suite of AI workflow tools. The acquisition strengthens the company's automation and agent-building capabilities.

cards.title: Anthropic Raises $65B at $965B Valuation
cards.body: Anthropic closed a massive $65 billion Series H funding round, valuing the AI startup at $965 billion. This could be the company's final private raise before its anticipated IPO.

cards.title: AI Tokens Becoming Tradeable Commodity Futures
cards.body: Major exchanges are designing derivative products around AI tokens as raw material inputs. AI tokens are increasingly viewed like gold, oil, electricity, or bandwidth.

cards.title: Pope's Encyclical Addresses Artificial Intelligence
cards.body: Pope Leo XIV's encyclical Magnifica Humanitas emphasizes that 'technology is never neutral' regarding AI. The document calls for courage and solidarity as society navigates the AI moment.

cards.title: Rethinking Organizations for Agentic AI Adoption
cards.body: While 85% of organizations want agentic AI within three years, 76% lack operational infrastructure to support it. A major disconnect exists between AI adoption ambitions and execution capabilities.

cards.title: AI Jobs Panic Lacks Real Evidence, Study Shows
cards.body: Despite growing hysteria about AI threatening white-collar employment, concrete evidence of mass job displacement remains scarce. Reality checks are needed on AI's actual labor market impact.

cards.title: LLMs Believe False Statements Despite Warnings
cards.body: Large language models continue accepting false information even after explicit corrections and warnings. This fundamental limitation raises concerns about LLM reliability and truthfulness.

cards.title: Developer Injects Data-Destroying Prompt Injection Attack
cards.body: A frustrated developer embedded a prompt injection attack into code written by less rigorous 'vibe coders.' The malicious prompt could destroy data, highlighting AI-generated code security risks.

cards.title: Home Cleaning Startup Offers Free Service for Robot Training
cards.body: A startup offers free home cleaning services in exchange for recording footage to train cleaning robots. The data collection approach enables AI training for autonomous home service robots.
